completed_work: Updated ask_provider.py to use a task label for latest-context assembly.
Updated compare_providers.py to use a task label for latest-context assembly.
Added tests proving long prompts are shortened for context assembly but preserved for provider prompts.
key_decisions: Separate context selection labels from provider-facing prompt content.
Do not relax the ContextPackManifest.task length limit; instead, derive a safe task label.
next_actions: Consider optional admission gating after the context task-label separation is safely recorded.
open_questions: Should context manifests later store both task_label and full_prompt_hash explicitly?
risks: The shortened task label may lose some semantic detail for context assembly unless later paired with prompt hashing or richer task metadata.
summary_text: AI-Lab fixed the latest-context prompt-length failure mode by separating context manifest task labels from full provider prompts. A new context_task_label helper normalizes and truncates long prompts to a manifest-safe label of at most 500 characters. ask_provider.py and compare_providers.py now use this short label for latest-context manifest assembly while preserving the full raw prompt in the provider-facing User task section. The fix was verified with a 1200-character prompt, passed the full test suite, and was committed as ce67a92. This preserves provider intent while preventing ContextPackManifest.task validation failures on long prompts.
